summary: Russia launched a large-scale overnight attack on Ukraine with 2 Onyx/anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with hits recorded at 11 locations. Russian forces also struck Kyiv with ballistic missiles and deployed strike drones across multiple oblasts including Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Chernihiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Kharkiv regions. Ground operations continue in Donetsk region with Russian forces advancing toward Kramatorsk and Slavyansk.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight with 2 Onyx missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with impacts at 11 locations and debris at 13 additional locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).

Russian ballistic missiles targeted Kyiv's Darnytsia district, with fires reported at multiple addresses including a residential building and a service station, injuring two people (Source: @UkraineNow/82231).
Strike drones were reported across Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Chernihiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Kharkiv oblasts throughout the day, with multiple waves targeting various cities (Source: @eRadarrua/98467).
Russia deployed KAB guided aviation bombs against Zaporizhzhia and Sumy regions, with three injured including a child in Zaporizhzhia from KAB strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/82238).
Ukraine's Armed Forces position in Donetsk region continues to deteriorate with Russian forces advancing toward Kramatorsk and Slavyansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214).
At the G7 summit, Trump raised the possibility of revising the Anchorage agreements on Ukraine, indicating potential pressure on Russia (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31215).
Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing in 2026-2029 according to Finance Minister Sergey Marchenko (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).
Russia's MoD reported a Geran strike on a training camp of the Special Operations Center 'Yug' of the Armed Forces of Ukraine in Mykolaiv region (Source: @tass_agency/382747).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a large-scale overnight attack on Ukraine with 2 Onyx/anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with hits recorded at 11 locations. Russian forces also struck Kyiv with ballistic missiles and deployed strike drones across multiple oblasts including Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Chernihiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Kharkiv regions. Ground operations continue in Donetsk region with Russian forces advancing toward Kramatorsk and Slavyansk.

points: Ukraine's General Staff reported that overnight Russia launched 2 Onyx/anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones at Ukraine, with impacts recorded at 11 locations and falling debris at 13 additional locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).

Seven Ukrainian civilians who were illegally held captive in Russia for years were successfully returned home; Presidential Commissioner for the Release of Prisoners Lubynets stated approximately 16,000 Ukrainian civilians may remain in Russian captivity (Source: @UkraineNow/82230).

Ukraine's Finance Minister Sergey Marchenko announced following meetings with UK and Lithuanian finance ministers in Gdansk that Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing during 2026-2029 (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).

President Zelensky unveiled a bust of Hetman Ivan Mazepa at Kyiv Pechersk Lavra and announced a full monument to Mazepa will be installed in central Kyiv on the site where a Lenin statue was toppled in 2013 (Source: @UkraineNow/82239).

The Ukrainian Armed Forces' position in Donetsk region continues to deteriorate with Russian forces advancing toward Kramatorsk and Slavyansk, according to Bloomberg citing military analyst Michael Kofman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214).
Footage from Lutsk showed a man being pushed into a van during mobilization efforts; city residents attempted to intervene but were unsuccessful (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31220).
Russia's Ministry of Defense reported a Geran drone strike on a training camp of the Special Operations Center 'Yug' of the Armed Forces of Ukraine in Mykolaiv region (Source: @tass_agency/382747).
Russian tactical aviation deployed KAB guided aviation bombs against targets in Zaporizhzhia and Sumy regions (Source: @kpszsu/66644).
Russian strike drones of the 'Banderol' type were launched from a northern direction toward targets in Sumy region (Source: @eRadarrua/98497).
A Russian ballistic missile was reported targeting Chuhuyiv in Kharkiv region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68136).
High threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region toward Ukraine was reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145).
Russia's overnight strike package included 2 Onyx/anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).

Ukraine's Armed Forces position in Donetsk region continues to deteriorate with Russian forces advancing toward Kramatorsk and Slavyansk, according to Bloomberg citing military analyst Michael Kofman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214).
Russian tactical aviation launched KAB guided aviation bomb strikes on targets in eastern Ukraine including Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @kpszsu/66644).
A Russian ballistic missile was reported targeting Chuhuyiv in Kharkiv region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68136).
Strike drones were reported moving from Kharkiv region toward Poltava region, passing near Krasny Kutsk (Source: @eRadarrua/98508).
Russian forces deployed KAB guided bombs on the eastern front targeting Zaporizhzhia and Sumy regions (Source: @kpszsu/66644).
Strike drones were reported on the border of Sumy and Chernihiv regions heading southward, with additional drones heading toward Sumy from both north and south (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
A strike drone was reported in the suburbs of Chernihiv city and another near Koriukivka in Chernihiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/98486).
A strike drone was reported heading from Sumy region toward Chernihiv region in the direction of Novhorod-Siverskyi (Source: @kpszsu/66659).
Reactive strike drones were reported in northern Chernihiv region heading westward (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
A pair of strike drones passed near Nikopol heading toward Sofiyivka in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @eRadarrua/98492).
Russia dropped KAB guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia city, injuring three people including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82238).
Russian MoD reported a Geran drone strike on a training camp of Special Operations Center 'Yug' of the Armed Forces of Ukraine in Mykolaiv region (Source: @tass_agency/382747).
Strike drones were reported in Mykolaiv region heading toward Voznesensk (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
Russian forces struck Zaporizhzhia region with KAB guided bombs, injuring three including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82238).
A pair of strike drones passed near Nikopol heading toward Sofiyivka (Source: @eRadarrua/98492).

Russia's overnight attack package included 2 Onyx/anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with impacts at 11 locations and debris at 13 additional locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).
Two Onyx missiles were reported heading toward Kyiv, with one reactive drone reported in Darnytsia district (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68098).
High threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region was reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145).
Strike drones of the 'Banderol' type were launched from a northern direction (Source: @eRadarrua/98497).
A Russian ballistic missile was reported targeting Chuhuyiv in Kharkiv region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68136).
Reactive strike drones were reported heading toward Terny in Sumy region (Source: @eRadarrua/98516).

key_developments: Russia launched a massive overnight attack with 2 Onyx missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with impacts at 11 locations and debris at 13 additional locations across Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).

Russian ballistic missiles struck Kyiv's Darnytsia district causing fires at multiple addresses including a residential building and service station, with two people reported injured (Source: @UkraineNow/82231).
Russia deployed KAB guided aviation bombs against Zaporizhzhia city, injuring three people including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82238).
Ukraine's Armed Forces position in Donetsk region continues to deteriorate with Russian forces advancing toward Kramatorsk and Slavyansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214).
At the G7 summit, Trump raised the possibility of revising the Anchorage agreements on Ukraine, indicating potential pressure on Russia (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31215).
Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing during 2026-2029 according to Finance Minister Sergey Marchenko (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).
Russia's MoD reported a Geran drone strike on a training camp of Special Operations Center 'Yug' of the Armed Forces of Ukraine in Mykolaiv region (Source: @tass_agency/382747).
sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment is characterized by sustained large-scale Russian strikes across multiple axes. The overnight launch of 142 drones alongside 8 missiles demonstrates Russia's continued capacity for massed long-range strikes. The deployment of KAB guided aviation bombs in Zaporizhzhia and Sumy regions indicates active employment of air-delivered precision munitions. The deteriorating situation in Donetsk region with Russian forces approaching Kramatorsk and Slavyansk represents the most significant ground threat. Multiple ballistic missile alerts targeting Kyiv, Chuhuyiv, and other cities indicate no reduction in strategic strike operations.
sections.regional_response: At the G7 summit, Trump raised the possibility of revising the Anchorage agreements on Ukraine, signaling a potential shift in US diplomatic posture toward Russia. Finland's President Stubb stated that neither sanctions nor Russian military losses will end the war, but that domestic unrest in Russia could compel Putin to stop. Poland's President Nawrocki saw his approval rating rise to 54.8% after Zelensky was stripped of the Order of the White Eagle. Ukraine's Finance Minister Marchenko secured engagement with UK and Lithuanian counterparts in Gdansk regarding the $146 billion external financing need for 2026-2029.
sections.intelligence_notes: The source messages cover a 24-hour period on June 27-28, 2026. Multiple independent channels corroborate the large-scale overnight drone and missile attack. The messages contain a mix of operational military reporting, political commentary, and non-Ukraine conflict items that have been filtered per relevance rules. Some channels cited are Russian state media (TASS) and may reflect official Russian positions. The DeepStateUA post references FOBOS unit operations but the message is truncated and lacks specific operational details. The eRadarrua channel provides the most granular real-time drone tracking data across multiple oblasts.
